resume.title: Software Engineer II
resume.headline: Software Engineer II with 5+ years in cloud infrastructure, IoT platforms, and distributed systems.
resume.summary: Software Engineer II with experience building and operating globally distributed cloud services, infrastructure tooling, and customer-facing IoT platforms at Microsoft. Strong background in TypeScript, C#, .NET, Go, Kubernetes, Azure, CI/CD, reliability, and cross-team collaboration.

work_experience.title: Software Engineer II
bullets:
Owned Infrastructure for a globally distributed service of 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices (TypeScript/.NET/Go)
running on AKS (Kubernetes) clusters – utilized Docker, Helm, Istio, and related technologies in the space.

Heavily involved in PROD incident response across all service team areas, offered solutions for hot-fixing and
minimizing customer impact and service downtime.

Developed tooling and documentation to democratize Infrastructure operations to engineers across all service teams,
eliminating dev cycle bottlenecks.
Continually maintained service compliance with standards such as GDPR and FedRAMP.
Overhauled CI/CD pipelines, enabled targeted microservice deployment, tripled deployment frequency.
Implemented ‘region agnostic’ environment provisioning, reducing the time to service presence in a new region from weeks to hours.

Saved hundreds of thousands in dev cost through solutions such as ’self-serve’ dev environment provisioning in ‘leased’
subscriptions - effectively eliminating cost of unused resources.
Mentored junior engineers and fostered an environment to comfortably onboard, ask questions, and grow.

work_experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets:
Full-Stack engineer for a service that provisioned packaged ‘IoT Solutions’ and resources in the customer’s Azure
cloud subscription.

Spearheaded a UX/UI refresh with a focus on Accessibility during a time crunch after the existing Front-End engineers
had moved to a different team.

Drastically improved service reliability and customer experience by identifying and eliminating the most common
failure scenarios.

Adopted a RESTful based deployment engine to replace code that had been tightly coupled to the service’s UI, greatly
improving code maintainability.
Developed a custom testing application in Python to continually validate our E2E deployment scenario.

Delivered weekly incident reports to IoT leadership on behalf of multiple teams based in Washington and Germany,
and one third-party offering from Intel.
Gracefully deprecated the service over a 6-month period while working on a new team.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

repos.name: tailord
repos.readme_summary: Tailord generates structured, role-specific "Tailorings" that explain why a candidate is a strong fit for a specific job description. It uses a multi-stage LLM pipeline to scrape job postings, extract requirements, score candidate experience, generate an advocacy letter, and stream progress to the client as each stage completes.

experience.raw_resume_text:
Charles Dunbar
dunbarchara@gmail.com • linkedin.com/in/charles-dunbar

Experience

Microsoft – Software Engineer II
 Azure IoT Central

 Redmond, WA

01/2020 - 04/2023

• Owned Infrastructure for a globally distributed service of 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices (TypeScript/.NET/Go)

running on AKS (Kubernetes) clusters – utilized Docker, Helm, Istio, and related technologies in the space.

• Heavily involved in PROD incident response across all service team areas, offered solutions for hot-fixing and

minimizing customer impact and service downtime.

• Developed tooling and documentation to democratize Infrastructure operations to engineers across all service teams,

eliminating dev cycle bottlenecks.

• Continually maintained service compliance with standards such as GDPR and FedRAMP.
• Overhauled CI/CD pipelines, enabled targeted microservice deployment, tripled deployment frequency.
• Implemented ‘region agnostic’ environment provisioning, reducing the time to service presence in a new region from weeks to hours.

• Saved hundreds of thousands in dev cost through solutions such as ’self-serve’ dev environment provisioning in ‘leased’

subscriptions - effectively eliminating cost of unused resources.

• Mentored junior engineers and fostered an environment to comfortably onboard, ask questions, and grow.

 Azure IoT Solutions

03/2018 - 12/2019

• Full-Stack engineer for a service that provisioned packaged ‘IoT Solutions’ and resources in the customer’s Azure

cloud subscription.

• Spearheaded a UX/UI refresh with a focus on Accessibility during a time crunch after the existing Front-End engineers

had moved to a different team.

• Drastically improved service reliability and customer experience by identifying and eliminating the most common

failure scenarios.

• Adopted a RESTful based deployment engine to replace code that had been tightly coupled to the service’s UI, greatly

improving code maintainability.

• Developed a custom testing application in Python to continually validate our E2E deployment scenario.
• Delivered weekly incident reports to IoT leadership on behalf of multiple teams based in Washington and Germany,

and one third-party offering from Intel.

• Gracefully deprecated the service over a 6-month period while working on a new team.

Java • TypeScript • C# • Node.js • Azure Cloud • Kubernetes • Docker • Helm • DevOps • CI/CD

Skills

Education

University of Arizona – Tucson, AZ

Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

GPA • 3.8 (Magna Cum Laude)
Graduation • December 2017

Hobbies

Fitness • Sports • Arduino • 3D Modeling/Printing • Videography • Art • DIY

8bf6bf16-be04-4356-a558-578a31b7ca40.generated_output:
Hello **Vercel**,

Given the requirements in your [Staff Engineer](http://localhost:3000/mock/job-staff-engineer) job posting, here are some reasons **Charles Dunbar** would be a strong fit for the role.

---

**Built and operated globally distributed cloud services at Microsoft scale**

Charles Dunbar brings direct experience owning infrastructure for a globally distributed service with 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices running on AKS/Kubernetes. His work spans Docker, Helm, Istio, CI/CD, and reliability, with hands-on incident response and compliance responsibilities in a production environment. That background maps closely to the kind of infrastructure judgment Vercel needs for edge and platform work. *[Resume]*

**Demonstrated Next.js experience through a public full-stack product**

His public project, Tailord, is built with Next.js, React, TypeScript, and NextAuth.js, showing current, practical use of the framework in a shipped application. The repository also includes a modern supporting stack—FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Terraform, Azure Container Apps, AWS, and GitHub Actions—which suggests comfort working across the full delivery path rather than only in the frontend. For a Staff Engineer role at Vercel, that combination is a credible signal of hands-on Next.js fluency. *[GitHub]*

**Cross-team leadership grounded in operational ownership**

At Microsoft, he did more than execute within a single squad: he delivered weekly incident reports to IoT leadership on behalf of multiple teams, developed tooling to democratize infrastructure operations across service teams, and mentored junior engineers. He also overhauled CI/CD pipelines to increase deployment frequency and implemented region-agnostic provisioning that reduced new-region setup from weeks to hours. Those are the kinds of leverage points that matter when a Staff Engineer is expected to improve how multiple teams build and ship. *[Resume]*

**Strong evidence of platform thinking and developer-experience improvement**

His resume shows repeated focus on removing bottlenecks for other engineers: self-serve environment provisioning, documentation that opened infrastructure operations to more teams, and targeted deployment tooling that improved release velocity. He also worked on service reliability, customer impact reduction, and maintainability by decoupling deployment logic from the UI in an earlier role. That pattern suggests someone who thinks in systems and developer experience, not just individual features. *[Resume]*

---

Charles Dunbar presents as a strong fit for a Staff Engineer conversation at Vercel because he combines Next.js experience with substantial cloud infrastructure ownership and cross-team operational leadership. His background suggests he can contribute credibly to both product-facing execution and the platform-level decisions that shape how teams ship.

If you're interested in continuing the conversation, Charles can be reached at [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com).

---

*Charles Dunbar · Software Engineer II · [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com) · [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/charles-dunbar)*

gaps.job_requirement: Contributions to frontend performance or Core Web Vitals at scale
gaps.question_for_candidate: Vercel cares a lot about shipping fast, low-latency web experiences—have you ever owned a frontend performance or Core Web Vitals improvement at scale, such as reducing LCP/INP/CLS on a production app, and what did you change?
gaps.context: This matters for a Staff Engineer at Vercel because the role is centered on helping teams build and ship web experiences that stay fast and Core Web Vitals-friendly at scale.

f2ac043a-3a75-4e8b-ac4d-7fe717fe4f53.generated_output:
Hello **Stripe**,

Given the requirements in your [Senior Software Engineer](http://localhost:3000/mock/job-mid-level-engineer) job posting, here are some reasons **Charles Dunbar** would be a strong fit for the role.

---

**Five years building and operating production cloud services at Microsoft**

Charles Dunbar brings five years of professional software engineering experience, all in Microsoft roles focused on Azure IoT and customer-facing cloud systems. He owned infrastructure for a globally distributed service with 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices, and he was heavily involved in production incident response and service reliability work. That combination gives Stripe a candidate who has already worked in the kind of high-ownership, production environment where correctness and uptime matter. *[Resume]*

**Deep backend strength across TypeScript, Go, Python, and RESTful services**

His technical background is anchored in TypeScript, Go, and Python, with production work spanning TypeScript/.NET/Go services and a Python testing application. He also adopted a RESTful deployment engine to replace UI-coupled code, improving maintainability in a live service. This is a strong fit for a team that needs someone comfortable shipping backend systems and working across service boundaries. *[Resume]*

**Proven experience with containers, Kubernetes, and deployment automation**

Charles has hands-on experience with Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, AKS, Istio, and CI/CD from operating Microsoft services at scale. He overhauled deployment pipelines, enabled targeted microservice deployment, and tripled deployment frequency, while also building region-agnostic provisioning that cut new-region setup from weeks to hours. That history suggests he can contribute quickly to infrastructure-heavy product engineering work. *[Resume]*

**Evidence of mentorship, reliability work, and practical frontend exposure**

He has mentored junior engineers and helped create an environment where teammates could onboard, ask questions, and grow. Earlier in his Microsoft work, he also led a UX/UI refresh with an accessibility focus and used a public Next.js/React project to build a full-stack AI-powered tailoring platform backed by PostgreSQL and SQLAlchemy. Together, those signals show a candidate who can collaborate across the stack and support the team around him. *[Resume] [GitHub]*

---

Charles looks like a strong match for a Senior Software Engineer role that values production ownership, backend depth, and operational maturity. His background suggests he can contribute credibly to Stripe’s engineering bar while also helping improve the systems and practices around the team.

If you're interested in continuing the conversation, Charles can be reached at [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com).

---

*Charles Dunbar · Software Engineer II · [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com) · [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/charles-dunbar)*

gaps.job_requirement: Payments or financial services domain experience
gaps.question_for_candidate: Stripe is a payments company—have you worked on any payments, billing, checkout, fraud, or other financial-services systems where you owned a concrete piece of the product or platform?
gaps.context: This matters at Stripe because Senior Software Engineers often build systems directly in the payments domain, where domain knowledge affects correctness, compliance, and customer impact.

Hello **Linear**,

Given the requirements in your [Software Engineer](http://localhost:3000/mock/job-software-engineer-linear) job posting, here are some reasons **Charles Dunbar** would be a strong fit for the role.

---

**TypeScript across distributed services and product UI**

Charles brings direct TypeScript experience on both sides of the stack. At Microsoft, he built and operated globally distributed services using TypeScript/.NET/Go, and his Tailord project uses TypeScript in a Next.js and React application with a FastAPI backend. That combination maps well to a team that values shipping cohesive product work across frontend and backend boundaries. *[Resume] [GitHub]*

**Relational data and API-driven product engineering**

His GitHub project shows hands-on PostgreSQL and SQLAlchemy usage, giving him direct relational database experience. On the Microsoft side, he also worked with RESTful APIs and even replaced tightly coupled UI logic with a RESTful deployment engine, which suggests comfort designing maintainable service interfaces rather than just consuming them. *[Resume] [GitHub]*

**React component work with a strong product-quality mindset**

Tailord demonstrates React and Next.js component-driven UI development, while his Microsoft work included a UX/UI refresh with an explicit focus on accessibility. He also improved reliability and customer experience, and maintained compliance with GDPR and FedRAMP, which points to an engineer who pays attention to the details that shape product trust and polish. *[Resume] [GitHub]*

**Experience shipping productivity-oriented tooling**

Charles has already built tooling that reduces friction for other engineers, including documentation and infrastructure tooling to eliminate dev-cycle bottlenecks at Microsoft. His Tailord project is also a productivity tool by design, automating a multi-stage workflow to generate structured, role-specific application materials. That background should translate well to a product like Linear, where thoughtful tooling and workflow improvements matter. *[Resume] [GitHub]*

---

Charles pairs full-stack TypeScript work with real experience improving developer workflows, service reliability, and product quality in production environments. He looks like a credible fit for a conversation with Linear because he has already shipped the kind of practical, detail-oriented software that makes teams faster and users happier.

If you're interested in continuing the conversation, Charles can be reached at [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com).

---

*Charles Dunbar · Software Engineer II · [dunbarchara@gmail.com](mailto:dunbarchara@gmail.com) · [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/charles-dunbar)*

gaps.question_for_candidate: Linear’s product is API-first, so can you share a concrete example where you designed and implemented a GraphQL API end-to-end, including schema design, resolvers, and pagination/error handling?
gaps.context: This matters for a Software Engineer at Linear because the product’s client experience depends on a well-designed GraphQL layer that stays fast, consistent, and easy for frontend teams to consume.

gaps.question_for_candidate: Linear’s desktop app work often touches Electron-specific patterns like native menus, shortcuts, deep-linking, auto-updates, or IPC—have you shipped a desktop app or Electron feature where you owned one of those end-to-end?
gaps.context: This matters for a Software Engineer at Linear because desktop app work in Electron has different constraints and integration points than web-only engineering, and they need evidence you can build and ship in that environment.

chunks.match_rationale: [vector top-8] Job title, not a requirement.

chunks.content: About the role
chunks.match_rationale: Section header

chunks.content: Vercel is the platform for frontend developers. We run one of the largest edge networks in the world, serving millions of deployments per day for teams at companies of every size. We are looking for a Staff Engineer to help define the future of our edge infrastructure and runtime environments.
chunks.match_rationale: [vector top-8] Charles has strong infrastructure and distributed-systems experience from Azure IoT Central, including ownership of globally distributed services on AKS with Docker, Helm, and Istio, which is adjacent to edge infrastructure and runtime work. However, there is no explicit evidence of edge networking, CDN/edge runtime systems, or Vercel-like frontend platform infrastructure.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has owned infrastructure for a globally distributed Azure service and worked hands-on with Kubernetes, Docker, Helm, and Istio at Microsoft. That background gives him relevant scale and runtime-infrastructure experience, even though his resume does not explicitly show edge-network or frontend-platform work.

chunks.content: This is a technical leadership role. You will set architectural direction, drive initiatives across multiple engineering teams, and have a measurable impact on the reliability and performance of our global platform. You will be a multiplier — making the engineers around you more effective and helping Vercel stay ahead of the frontier.
chunks.match_rationale: [vector top-8] Charles has direct experience setting infrastructure direction and driving work across multiple teams at Microsoft Azure IoT Central, including tooling that democratized infrastructure operations across service teams and ownership of infrastructure for a globally distributed service with 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices. His incident response and reliability work on a global platform also shows measurable impact on service stability and performance.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has already operated in a technical leadership capacity at Microsoft, owning infrastructure for a globally distributed service and building tooling that made engineers across service teams more effective. His work on incident response, hot-fixing, and reducing customer impact shows the kind of cross-team reliability leadership Vercel is looking for.

chunks.content: About the role
chunks.match_rationale: Section header

chunks.content: We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our Payments Infrastructure team. You will build and scale the systems that move money reliably for millions of businesses around the world. This is a high-ownership role: you will be responsible for the full lifecycle of the services you build, from design through production.
chunks.match_rationale: [vector top-8] Charles has strong experience building and operating globally distributed cloud services and infrastructure at Microsoft, including ownership of Azure IoT Central infrastructure, CI/CD, and reliability work. However, the profile does not show direct payments systems or money-movement infrastructure experience, so this is adjacent rather than a direct match for Payments Infrastructure.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles brings senior-level ownership of large-scale cloud infrastructure from Microsoft, including a globally distributed service with 35+ engineers and 40+ microservices, plus CI/CD and reliability improvements. While his background is not specifically in payments, he has the kind of production-scale systems experience that translates well to a high-ownership infrastructure role.

However, there is no explicit evidence of payment processing or financial infrastructure work, so this is adjacent rather than a full match.

chunks.content: About the role
chunks.match_rationale: Section header

chunks.content: Linear is building the next generation of project management software. We care deeply about product quality, performance, and developer experience. We're looking for a Software Engineer who sweats the details, ships fast, and wants to work on a product used by tens of thousands of engineering teams worldwide.
chunks.match_rationale: [vector top-8] Charles has strong experience building and operating software products, including customer-facing Azure IoT services and globally distributed infrastructure, but the profile does not explicitly show project management software or direct evidence of obsessing over product quality and developer experience in a product like Linear.
chunks.advocacy_blurb: Charles has shipped and operated customer-facing cloud products at Microsoft, including Azure IoT Solutions and Azure IoT Central, where he built tooling to remove dev-cycle bottlenecks and improve infrastructure operations for engineers. While his background is not in project management software specifically, he brings relevant experience with fast-moving, detail-oriented product and platform work.

chunks.content: This is a full-stack role. You'll work across our TypeScript backend and React frontend, contribute to our public GraphQL API, and help shape how Linear evolves as a product.
chunks.match_rationale: [vector top-8] Charles has direct TypeScript and React experience from both Microsoft and GitHub, and he has worked as a full-stack engineer. However, there is no explicit evidence of public GraphQL API work, so this is a partial match rather than a full one.

He is a credible fit for the frontend/backend stack, though his profile does not explicitly show GraphQL API ownership.
